behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the PR file-discovery path used by a GitHub Action, which commonly depends on real git checkout/diff behavior and filesystem state. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local filtering logic; it is changed-file handoff into analysis scope. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked changed-file list can hide real failures from git diff semantics (renames, path normalization, shallow fetch edge cases). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text says the step "reads all changed files in a pull request," which keeps real PR diff acquisition in scope, so higher level is retained.
behaviors.test_description: Run the lint step in a real CI-like job against a test repo with a PR containing changed + unchanged files (include a fixed hard-case canary set: rename, deleted file, nested path). Assert only changed files are passed to analysis and unchanged files are never scanned.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is module handoff: analyzer outputs findings, formatter groups by severity, publisher receives composed comment body. No required real OS/network/subprocess to validate grouping correctness. REAL-INFRA check: posting can be stubbed while still verifying payload structure; no need for real GitHub API to catch grouping bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text focuses on "grouped by severity" rather than API transport/rendering correctness, so Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire analyzer-result model + comment formatter + PR-comment publisher adapter with an in-process fake GitHub client. Feed mixed severities and assert generated comment payload has distinct error/warning/info sections with correct membership/order.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is runtime performance under CI constraints (process scheduling, I/O throughput, linter execution time), which is real infrastructure dependent. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing or fake linter calls can pass while real execution exceeds timeout. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly targets "10-minute Actions timeout," an OS/CI execution boundary, so System is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the actual lint action end-to-end on a repo fixture with 200 changed files (fixed hard-case canary mix included) in a CI-like environment; measure wall-clock completion and assert finish under 10 minutes with findings posted.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly includes "linter process crashes," which is subprocess/OS-failure handling. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: replacing crash with an in-process exception can miss true crash semantics (exit code/signal, partial output, process isolation). Requires verifying recovery path continues with remaining files and posts final comment. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but concrete text "process crashes" disqualifies lower level.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real linter subprocess configured to crash on a crafted malformed canary file. Assert pipeline records the crash, continues analyzing subsequent files, and still publishes a PR comment containing remaining findings plus crash notice.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure is primarily deterministic dedup logic over finding keys (file/line/rule/message), with no required I/O boundary. MOCK-MISS check: behavior can be fully exercised as pure data transformation; no module or infra interaction is required to reproduce the defect. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered due "overlapping rules," but behavior text scopes assertion to "deduplicated in the output," which can be validated directly by the dedup function/object contract.
behaviors.test_description: Test dedup function/object with fixtures containing overlapping-rule duplicates on identical file+line and near-duplicates on different lines/rules. Assert exact dedup set and stable retained representative metadata.
